Ego Language-Annotation Atlas
中文

EPIC-KITCHENS-100

100 hours of unscripted egocentric kitchen activity, densely annotated as verb plus noun action segments parsed from participant narrations.

annotations repo · paper · project page · license: CC BY-NC 4.0

Hand-compiled reference over downloaded data, may contain errors. Open an issue to fix it.

01Data-usage path

EPIC-KITCHENS-100 records 100 hours of unscripted egocentric kitchen activity, verified here at 100.03 hours across 700 videos. After recording, participants narrate their own actions in free form, and each narration is parsed into a verb plus noun action segment with start and stop times. The 89,977 resulting segments, 76,885 of them carrying released narration text, define the dataset's recognition, detection, anticipation and retrieval benchmarks, and the corpus is widely reused as supervision for egocentric perception pretraining.

raw egocentric video
→
free-form narration
→
verb + noun + timestamps
→
recognition / detection / anticipation / retrieval
→
downstream: perception pretraining

02Annotation clusters

Each point is one unique cleaned annotation, positioned by UMAP over sentence-embeddings and colored by its cluster's verb family. Hover for the string, search to highlight, click a legend family to toggle it, or click a cluster row below.

color: verb family
highlighting cluster:

07Provenance and reproducibility

source https://github.com/epic-kitchens/epic-kitchens-100-annotations
annotations commit ea8b40457a400c3fffa1c7f406ef3dc169cc2522
dataset version EPIC-KITCHENS-100 @ ea8b40457a
access date 2026-06-29
build date 2026-06-28
encoder sentence-transformers/all-MiniLM-L6-v2
reducer UMAP viz(2D, n_neighbors=15, min_dist=0.1, cosine), cluster(5D)
clustering HDBSCAN(min_cluster_size=50, min_samples=10), 111 clusters, noise 15.9%
license CC BY-NC 4.0
attribution Damen et al., Rescaling Egocentric Vision, IJCV 2022 (arXiv:2006.13256).
compiled for Ego Language-Annotation Atlas

cited: Per the paper: 45 kitchens, about 20M frames, 90k action segments. This atlas measures 100.03 hours, 700 videos, 76,885 labelled segments, 34 participants in the released train and validation splits.

Every statistic traces to the downloaded annotation files or is marked as cited. Cluster labels are auto-derived from head verbs and nouns, not hand-written. Unknown values are shown as n/a.
